name: VectorChord
tagline: Disk-friendly, billion-scale vector search inside PostgreSQL
seoTitle: VectorChord - Scalable Vector Search for PostgreSQL
metaDescription: VectorChord is a PostgreSQL extension for disk-friendly vector search. It's compatible with pgvector types and syntax and scales to billions of vectors.
overview: VectorChord (vchord) is a PostgreSQL extension for vector similarity search. It is the successor to pgvecto.rs and works with the same vector data types and SQL syntax as pgvector, so existing pgvector tables and queries keep working. It uses RaBitQ compression with automatic reranking to store large numbers of vectors cheaply while keeping search quality high.

It is aimed at teams who already run PostgreSQL and want to keep their vector data in the same database instead of adding a separate vector store. Because it reuses pgvector's `vector` type and the standard `ORDER BY embedding <-> ...` distance query, migrating an application is mostly a matter of installing the extension and creating a new index.

Within the vector-database category, VectorChord focuses on disk-based storage and large scale: the project reports hosting 100M vectors on a single AWS instance and building 100M-vector indexes in around 20 minutes, with index sizes that fit in memory budgets typical of one machine.
features: Compatible with pgvector data types and syntax, so existing tables and queries migrate with little change
RaBitQ compression plus automatic reranking to store many more vectors per dollar of storage
The `vchordrq` index type for approximate nearest-neighbor search on a vector column
Native low-bit vector types (4-bit RaBitQ4 and 8-bit RaBitQ8) to cut storage further
Disk-friendly design that targets 100M+ vectors on a single machine and scales toward 1B+
Fast index builds using hierarchical K-means and optimized disk operations

gettingStarted.intro: The quickest path is the official Docker image, then connect with psql and enable the extension.

steps.heading: Run the Docker image
steps.body: Start a PostgreSQL container that already includes the VectorChord extension.

steps.heading: Connect with psql
steps.body: Connect to the database. The default user is postgres and the password is the one you set above.

steps.heading: Enable the extension
steps.body: Create the vchord extension. CASCADE also pulls in pgvector, which VectorChord depends on.
steps.code: CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vchord CASCADE;

steps.heading: Create a table, index, and search
steps.body: Make a table with a vector column, build a vchordrq index, then run a nearest-neighbor query.

useCases: Adding semantic or similarity search to an app that already uses PostgreSQL, without running a separate vector database
Migrating from pgvector or pgvecto.rs while keeping the same vector types and SQL queries
Storing tens or hundreds of millions of embeddings on a single machine while controlling storage cost
Building a retrieval layer for RAG or recommendation features alongside existing relational data
